policy_statement: The Government of Armenia approved the Nationally Determined Contributions (2021-2030) with an unconditional target to reduce emissions by 40% below 1990 levels by 2030.
verbatim_text: On  April  22,  2021  the  Government  of Armenia  approved  Armenia's  Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) for a tenyear  implementation  period  (2021-2030), setting  the  new  unconditional  mitigation target of 40% reduction below 1990 emissions levels, to be achieved in 2030.
extraction_rationale: Contains a quantified national mitigation target with a deadline (40% below 1990 by 2030) approved by government.
reasoning: The verbatim text states that the Government of Armenia “approved” the NDC for 2021–2030 and sets an “unconditional mitigation target of 40% reduction below 1990 emissions levels, to be achieved in 2030.” This is an auditable, quantified outcome with a clear deadline and national scope. Language is strong (setting a target) and not merely aspirational.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: Explicit numeric outcome stated: “unconditional mitigation target of 40% reduction below 1990 emissions levels.” Timeline: Explicit deadline/year: “to be achieved in 2030” and “implementation period (2021-2030).” Binding mechanism: Text says “Government of Armenia approved… NDC” but does not specify an enforceable legal instrument (e.g., law/mandate/ordinance), so marked No. Spatial specificity: Applies at the national level (“Government of Armenia”, Armenia’s NDC), i.e., countrywide scope. Weak language: No terms like encourage/aim/explore; it states a set target. Strong language: “approved… setting the… target… to be achieved in 2030” plus hard numbers (40%, 1990 baseline, 2030). Strict valid rule: Meets gold standard (timeline Yes + quantifiable target Yes) and is an auditable commitment (mitigation target). Therefore VALID.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to construct about 1000 MW of solar power plants (including autonomous ones) by 2030 to support reaching solar generation targets.
verbatim_text: According  to  'The  Republic  of  Armenia Energy Sector Development Strategic Program (till 2040)", 2021, it is planned to increase the share of solar energy generation  in  total  to  at  least  15%  or  1.8 billion  kWh  by  2030.  To  this  end,  solar power  plants  with  the  capacity  of  about 1000 MW, including autonomous ones, are planned to be constructed.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure plan with quantified capacity (~1000 MW) and timeline (by 2030).
reasoning: The verbatim text contains a measurable outcome (construct ~1000 MW of solar capacity and raise solar generation share to at least 15% or 1.8 billion kWh) and a clear deadline (by 2030). However, it is framed as a plan (“it is planned… are planned to be constructed”) without an explicit binding mechanism (e.g., law/mandate). Under the strict rule, it still qualifies as VALID because it meets the gold standard (timeline + quantifiable target) and does not primarily rely on weak “encourage/explore” language.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text specifies measurable outcomes: "increase the share of solar energy generation... to at least 15% or 1.8 billion kWh by 2030" and "solar power plants with the capacity of about 1000 MW... are planned to be constructed." Timeline: Explicit deadline "by 2030." Binding mechanism: No enforceable instrument is cited; it is referenced as a strategic program and uses planning language ("it is planned"). Spatial specificity: Applies to "The Republic of Armenia" (national scope) as stated in the program title. Weak language: No 'encourage/explore/aim' phrasing; while 'planned' indicates intent, it is paired with hard numbers and a date. Strong language: Presence of hard numeric commitments tied to an action and deadline (construct ~1000 MW; reach 15%/1.8 billion kWh by 2030). Therefore, it satisfies the strict validity rule via timeline + quantifiable target, despite lacking an explicit binding mechanism.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to commission the Masrik-1 utility-scale solar PV plant (55 MW peak) by 2022 and hold tenders for additional solar PV plants totaling about 520 MW (including two 200 MW plants).
verbatim_text: It is planned by 2022 to commission Masrik-1 utility scale solar PV with a peak capacity  of  55  MW.  Further,  new  tenders will be held for the construction of another 7 solar PVs with a total installed capacity of about 520 MW, whereby the capacity of two of these plants will be 200 MW each.
extraction_rationale: Specific planned actions with clear capacities and a commissioning date plus procurement/tenders.
reasoning: The verbatim text contains a clear, auditable infrastructure commitment (commissioning a named solar PV plant and issuing tenders for additional plants) with explicit capacity figures (MW) and an explicit timeline (“by 2022”). It is spatially specific to Armenia by context, but does not specify subnational geography. Language is primarily commitment-oriented (“planned… to commission”, “tenders will be held”) rather than purely aspirational.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text specifies measurable capacity outcomes: “commission Masrik-1… peak capacity of 55 MW” and “another 7 solar PVs with a total installed capacity of about 520 MW… two… 200 MW each.” Timeline: An explicit deadline is given: “planned by 2022 to commission Masrik-1…”. Binding mechanism: No enforceable instrument (law/regulation/mandate) is mentioned; it is phrased as a plan (“It is planned…”, “tenders will be held”), so binding mechanism is not evidenced. Spatial specificity: The policy is country-scoped in context (Armenia) and references a specific project (Masrik-1), indicating applicability within that jurisdiction. Weak language: “It is planned…” indicates non-binding intent. Strong language: No mandate/require/achieve phrasing with compliance obligations is present; instead it uses planning/tender language. STRICT VALID RULE: (1) Auditable commitment is present (commissioning and tendering specific PV capacity); (2) Gold standard satisfied because both has_timeline=Yes and has_quantifiable_target=Yes; (3) Weak language is present but not disqualifying here because gold standard is met and the deliverables are concrete and auditable even without an explicit binding mechanism.

extraction_rationale: Quantified performance target (losses to 6.4%) with a deadline under an investment program.
reasoning: The verbatim text specifies a measurable outcome—distribution losses reduced to 6.4%—and an explicit deadline/year (2027), tied to a specific investment program (ENA 2016–2027). This makes the commitment auditable and time-bound. However, the excerpt does not explicitly state an enforceable mandate/regulatory instrument; it reads as a program target rather than a binding legal requirement. The policy statement uses “aims,” which is weak language, but the presence of a hard metric and deadline still supports validity under the strict rule because weak language can be tolerated when there is a concrete, auditable target and timeline (even without explicit binding mechanism).

Timeline: Yes — verbatim text includes "in 2027" (explicit year). Binding mechanism: No — text references an "Investment program" but does not indicate an enforceable law/regulation/mandate. Spatial specificity: No — no location/scope such as national/grid-region/service-territory is explicitly stated in the verbatim text. Weak language: Yes — policy statement says "aims to reduce" (aspirational phrasing). Strong language: No — no verbs like "require/mandate/shall" and no explicit enforcement language; only a target is listed. Strict rule check: (1) Auditable commitment passes due to clear deliverable (losses to 6.4%). (2) Gold standard passes (timeline Yes + quantifiable target Yes). (3) Language strength condition passes because a hard target + deadline is provided, though weak language remains and no binding mechanism is shown; overall treated as actionable/measurable based on the specific numeric target and date.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to reach total small hydropower plant (SHPP) capacity of 430 MW by 2023, supported by the Hydro Energy Development Concept and 15-year Power Purchase Agreements under the Energy Law.
verbatim_text:
In 2016 the GoA approved the Hydro Energy Development Concept of the RA, which envisages commissioning of new SHPPs. The Energy Law supports the development of SHPPs through the Power Purchasing Agreement for the 15- year period.

Quantitative goal: To reach the total capacity of SHPPs of 430 MWby 2023.
extraction_rationale: Clear policy support instrument (15-year PPA) and quantified capacity target with timeline.
reasoning: The verbatim text contains an explicit quantitative capacity target (“total capacity … 430 MW”) and a deadline (“by 2023”), plus a binding support mechanism referenced in law (15-year Power Purchase Agreements under the Energy Law). The commitment is auditable (installed/commissioned SHPP capacity can be measured) and is not merely a study/process statement. Therefore it meets the strict validity rule.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text states, "Quantitative goal: To reach the total capacity of SHPPs of 430 MW by 2023." This is a measurable outcome (MW capacity). Timeline: The same sentence includes an explicit deadline, "by 2023." Binding mechanism: The text grounds implementation support in enforceable instruments, "The Energy Law supports the development of SHPPs through the Power Purchasing Agreement for the 15-year period," and also notes government approval of a concept, "In 2016 the GoA approved the Hydro Energy Development Concept..." Spatial specificity: The policy applies to Armenia / the Republic of Armenia, indicated by "Hydro Energy Development Concept of the RA" and national institutions ("GoA"). Language strength: "approved" and the explicit "Quantitative goal" with hard numbers indicates strong, auditable intent; no purely aspirational verbs (e.g., encourage/aim) are used in place of targets. Strict rule check: (1) Auditable commitment satisfied via MW capacity deliverable; (2) Gold standard satisfied because both target and timeline are present; (3) Weak language not detected and a legal mechanism (Energy Law + PPAs) is present.

policy_statement: Under the Strategy and Action Plan, Armenia envisages construction of two 200 MW solar PV plants (400 MW total) in 2023 and 2024, including the 200 MW 'Ayg-1' plant.
verbatim_text:
According to the Strategy and Action Plan construction of 2 Solar PVs with the capacity of 200MW each is envisaged in 2023 and 2024.

Request for Qualification was announced in 2020 seeking private developers for a pro- ject to design, finance, build, own, and operate the first of considered 2 plants, which will be grid-connected 200MW solar PV power plant called 'Ayg-1'.

Despite that, the strict “gold standard” (timeline + quantifiable target) is met, so the statement can be treated as VALID, though with less than maximal confidence due to the non-binding language.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: Yes — "construction of 2 Solar PVs with the capacity of 200MW each" (implies 400 MW total). Timeline: Yes — "envisaged in 2023 and 2024." Binding mechanism: No — text describes plans/procurement ("envisaged"; "Request for Qualification was announced") but does not cite any enforceable mandate, law, or regulatory requirement. Spatial specificity: No — no location/jurisdictional scope stated beyond being grid-connected and Armenia context; the excerpt itself does not specify where it applies. Weak language: Yes — "is envisaged" signals intention rather than obligation. Strong language: No — no 'require/mandate/shall' style commitment with enforcement; although "construction" is an action, it is framed as envisaged rather than committed. STRICT VALID RULE: (1) auditable deliverable present (construction of two 200 MW PV plants); (2) gold standard satisfied (timeline + quantified capacity); (3) language strength condition satisfied because weak language is present but gold standard is met and the rule allows validity when timeline+target are met even without a binding mechanism; confidence reduced due to non-binding phrasing.

According to the Strategy and Action Plan, the construction of solar PV power plants with an installed capacity of up to5MW (inclusively) will be supported through tariff policy and power purchase agreement.

The quantitative goal is to support the installation of the total of 315 MWof solar PV power plants with an installed capacity of up to5MW (inclusively) by 2030.

The language indicates an implementable support mechanism (tariffs/PPA) rather than only aspirational intent.

Evidence: “…by 2030.” Binding mechanism: Not explicit as a legal mandate/ordinance; it describes support instruments (tariff policy, PPA) but does not state a requirement/mandate. Evidence: “will be supported through tariff policy and power purchase agreement.” Spatial specificity: Not specified (no explicit geography like nationwide/regions/citywide in the excerpt). Strong language: Detected because it sets a hard numeric target with a deadline (“315 MW… by 2030”) alongside implementation mechanisms (“tariff policy and power purchase agreement”).
